description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant with a domed top and a cylindrical body, commonly found in urban environments for emergency water access. The hydrant features three prominent hose connection outlets: one large central outlet on the front and two smaller outlets on either side. The top of the hydrant is capped with a rounded dome that has several raised ridges and a central operating nut. The base is wide and circular, bolted to the ground, and shows some signs of wear and grime, indicating outdoor use. The surface is painted a bright red, with some subtle weathering and dirt accumulation near the bottom. No chains or manufacturer markings are visible on the exterior.
description: A bright red, domed fire hydrant with three hose outlets and a bolted circular base, showing signs of outdoor wear. The surface is smooth and hard, with no visible chains or markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large central hose outlet and the domed top with the operating nut clearly visible.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting one of the smaller side hose outlets and the curvature of the hydrant body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the hydrant with both side outlets visible but no central outlet.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the other small hose outlet and the domed top from a different perspective.
